reasoning: KeyCorp has an ex-dividend date falling exactly after the forecast date on May 27, 2025. The baseline price against which resolution dates are compared is the close on May 23, 2025 ($14.965). The $0.205 per share dividend translates to an immediate 1.37% drop in the stock's opening price on May 27. The first resolution date is 2025-06-01, meaning the stock only has four trading days to overcome this gap in a negative-trending market (recent linear trend and 5-day momentum point downward). Therefore, the probability of exceeding the May 25 baseline price by June 1 rests at 0.35, reflecting the dividend handicap and recent short-term negative drift. For the subsequent resolution dates (June 24, August 23, November 21), uncertainty dominates, and the probability drifts progressively closer to the base rate of ~0.5. General long-term upward equity drift offsets some of the immediate downward pressures.
